articles.title: Federal judge in Boston bars Trump from implementing proof of citizenship requirement to vote
articles.description:
The ruling by U.S. District Court Judge Denise Casper in Boston effectively converts a preliminary injunction she issued a year ago, in which she temporarily blocked many of Trump’s efforts to overhaul elections, into a permanent ban.

The post Federal judge in Boston bars Trump from implementing proof of citizenship requirement to vote appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: U.S. District Court Judge Denise Casper issued a permanent ban on Trump's voting requirement.
The ruling converts a previous injunction from June 2022 into a lasting decision.
Boston residents will continue to vote without proof of citizenship requirements.

articles.title: Boston Dynamics seeks to double local headcount with Greater Boston expansion
articles.description:
The robotics company is building a new advanced robotics and AI center in Waltham and expects to double its local workforce.

The post Boston Dynamics seeks to double local headcount with Greater Boston expansion appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Boston Dynamics is expanding its operations with a new center in Waltham.
The company plans to double its local workforce from current levels.
This expansion will create significant job opportunities for Boston residents.

articles.title: 9-year-old seriously injured after falling from window in Hanson
articles.description:
The boy fell from a third-story window Wednesday afternoon, officials said.

The post 9-year-old seriously injured after falling from window in Hanson appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: A 9-year-old boy fell from a third-story window in Hanson on Wednesday afternoon.
The incident raised concerns about window safety in residential buildings in the area.
Local officials may consider new safety regulations to prevent similar accidents.

articles.title: Teso Life is opening a 20,000-square-foot Downtown Crossing store Friday
articles.description:
The popular Asian lifestyle chain is taking over the former Forever 21 space in Downtown Crossing.

The post Teso Life is opening a 20,000-square-foot Downtown Crossing store Friday appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Teso Life opens a 20,000-square-foot store in Downtown Crossing on Friday.
The store occupies the former Forever 21 location, enhancing local shopping options.
This expansion creates new jobs and boosts retail activity in Downtown Boston.

articles.title: Dianna Russini was an NFL ‘Insider.’ Was she also out of bounds?
articles.description:
Russini's approach to reporting raised specific questions, including whether her closeness to a source like Mike Vrabel compromised her role at the Athletic.

The post Dianna Russini was an NFL ‘Insider.’ Was she also out of bounds? appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Dianna Russini's reporting raised ethical questions about her ties to NFL coach Mike Vrabel.
Russini's closeness to sources may affect her credibility at The Athletic.
Boston sports fans may question the integrity of local sports journalism.

articles.title: Your next Uber or Lyft ride in Boston could be a taxi
articles.description:
A new pilot program will allow cab drivers to accept ride requests through ride-hailing apps for the first time.

The post Your next Uber or Lyft ride in Boston could be a taxi appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Boston launches a pilot program allowing taxi drivers to accept ride requests via apps.
The initiative aims to integrate traditional taxis into the ride-hailing market starting June 2026.
Residents may experience increased ride availability and potentially lower fares with more options.
